title: AI Architect Earning $1.1M/Year Almost Took on $550K in Debt to Dodge Taxes: ‘I Have No Idea What That Business Is’
excerpt: A tax advisory firm told a seven-figure earner he could wipe out his entire federal tax bill by borrowing half a million dollars to invest in a business he c...
content_html:
Michael Williams

Wed, July 22, 2026 at 7:04 PM CDT 4 min read

Quick Read

  • A tax shelter pitch offered an AI architect a $450K write-off by taking on $550K in debt, saving at most ~$200K in taxes.

  • The critical test for any tax shelter: would you invest without the write-off? If no, the tax benefit is bait, not a reason to invest.

  • Maxing out a Solo 401(k), SEP-IRA, or defined benefit plan reduces taxable income without borrowing a dollar or taking on unknown business risk.

  • Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

A caller on Ramsey's Everyday Millionaires segment recently walked through a pitch that should terrify anyone with a big tax bill. He is an AI architect who, with his wife, has earned roughly $1.1 million per year for the past four years and has been paying approximately $300,000 annually to the IRS. A tax advisory firm offered him a way out: take on $550,000 in debt, put in $85,000 as an initial investment, and use the remaining $450,000 as a tax write-off against his income. The firm claimed the deduction was legal under a new Trump-era tax provision and that "thousands" of taxpayers were already doing it.

The caller had a gut reaction that saved him. "I have no idea what that business is. That's why I was hesitant. I don't know whether it's going to be profitable or not." That instinct is the entire lesson.

The Verdict: This Pitch Is Dangerous, and the Math Proves It

The host was direct. "Run away from these people as fast as you possibly can." Here is why the arithmetic collapses.

A tax write-off reduces the income the IRS taxes, not the taxes you owe dollar for dollar. Someone in the top federal bracket pays 37% on marginal income. A $450,000 deduction saves roughly $166,500 in federal tax. Add a high-tax state and you might push combined savings toward $200,000. That is the ceiling of the reward.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

Now stack the risk. The caller would owe the lender the full $550,000 regardless of whether the underlying business earns a dime. At today's rates, that debt is expensive. The 10-year Treasury yield is near 4.5%, and small-business borrowing typically prices well above that benchmark. Interest alone on a half-million-dollar loan can run into the tens of thousands each year.

The host reframed the deal in a way every investor should memorize: "If you had $550,000, would you invest $550,000 cash in this in order to get a $450,000 write-off? Well, obviously then you have a net of $100,000 invested in a business that you don't know anything about. We wouldn't even do that, right? Much less have debt around it." Strip the tax veneer off and you are being asked to plow six figures into a black box.

迈克尔·威廉姆斯

2026 年 7 月 22 日星期三，美国中部时间 (CDT) 晚上 7:04 阅读时间 4 分钟

快速阅读

  • 一次避税宣传活动为一位 AI 建筑师提供了 45 万美元的冲销，他承担了 55 万美元的债务，最多节省了约 20 万美元的税款。
  • 对于任何避税天堂来说，关键的考验是：您会在没有冲销的情况下进行投资吗？ 如果不是，那么税收优惠只是诱饵，而不是投资的理由。
  • 充分利用 Solo 401(k)、SEP-IRA 或固定福利计划可以减少应税收入，而无需借入一美元或承担未知的商业风险。
  • 不要等待：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚透露了他最喜欢的 10 只人工智能股票。 立即免费查看完整列表。

拉姆齐《每日百万富翁》栏目中的一位来电者最近经历了一次推销，这应该会让任何拥有巨额税单的人感到恐惧。 他是一名人工智能架构师，与妻子在过去四年中每年赚取约 110 万美元，并每年向国税局缴纳约 30 万美元。 一家税务咨询公司为他提供了一条出路：承担 550,000 美元的债务，投入 85,000 美元作为初始投资，然后用剩余的 450,000 美元作为收入的税收冲销。 该公司声称，根据特朗普时代的新税收规定，这种扣除是合法的，并且“数千”纳税人已经在这样做。

打电话的人的本能反应救了他。 “我不知道那是什么生意。 这就是我犹豫的原因。 我不知道它是否能够盈利。” 这种本能就是整个课程。

结论：这个球场很危险，数学证明了这一点

主持人很直接。 “尽快逃离这些人。” 这就是算术崩溃的原因。

税收冲销减少的是国税局的税收收入，而不是您所欠的税款。 联邦最高阶层的人支付 37% 的边际收入。 450,000 美元的扣除额可节省大约 166,500 美元的联邦税。 添加一个高税收州，您可能会节省总计 200,000 美元。 这就是奖励的上限。

不要等待：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚透露了他最喜欢的 10 只人工智能股票。 立即免费查看完整列表。

现在堆积风险。 无论相关业务是否赚到一分钱，来电者都将欠贷方全额 550,000 美元。 按照今天的利率，这笔债务是昂贵的。 10 年期国债收益率接近 4.5%，小企业借款的价格通常远高于该基准。 每年仅五十万美元贷款的利息就可能达到数万美元。

主持人以每个投资者都应该记住的方式重新设计了这笔交易：“如果你有 55 万美元，你会投资 55 万美元现金以获得 45 万美元的冲销吗？ 嗯，很明显，你净投资了 10 万美元，投资于一家你一无所知的企业。 我们甚至不会那样做，对吧？ 与之相关的债务就更少了。” 剥掉税收外衣，你就会被要求将六位数的钱存入一个黑匣子。
